---
title: Quarto extensions
---

Extensions, filters, and shortcodes add authoring behavior beyond basic
Quarto Markdown. For new teaching-material projects, use the reusable
[`au-mbg/quarto-teaching-tools`](https://github.com/au-mbg/quarto-teaching-tools)
extensions rather than creating local equivalents.

## Teaching tools

| Extension | Role | Recommended use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `callout-solution` | Versioned answer callouts | Instructor-only explanations written as `.callout-solution` divs |
| `strip-solution` | Versioned divs and code cells | Blocks marked `teaching: exercise` or `teaching: solution` |
| `collapse-callouts` | Presentation helper | Set default collapsed state for standard callout types |
| `download-button` | Resource helper | Provide prominent downloadable files through a shortcode |

Install an extension from the repository root and commit the resulting
`_extensions/` contents:

```bash
quarto install extension au-mbg/quarto-teaching-tools/extensions/callout-solution
quarto install extension au-mbg/quarto-teaching-tools/extensions/strip-solution
quarto install extension au-mbg/quarto-teaching-tools/extensions/collapse-callouts
quarto install extension au-mbg/quarto-teaching-tools/extensions/download-button
```

Only install the extensions the project uses. Enable filters such as
`callout-solution`, `strip-solution`, and `collapse-callouts` in the project's
Quarto configuration.

## Versioning extensions

Both versioning extensions are controlled by profile metadata:

```yaml
teaching:
  show-solutions: true
  default-solution-title: "Solution"
```

`callout-solution` renders `.callout-solution` blocks as collapsed instructor
callouts only when solutions are shown. `strip-solution` keeps exercise blocks
for `show-solutions: false` and solution blocks for `show-solutions: true`.
When that setting is absent, `strip-solution` leaves both kinds of block in
the output.

For new content, use `teaching="exercise"` or `teaching="solution"` on divs,
and `#| teaching: exercise` or `#| teaching: solution` in code cells. Boolean
options such as `exercise: true` and `solution: true` are compatibility syntax
for existing content.

## Presentation and download extensions

`collapse-callouts` provides project-level defaults for ordinary Quarto
callouts:

```yaml
teaching:
  collapse-notes: true
  collapse-warnings: true
  collapse-importants: true
  collapse-tips: true
  collapse-cautions: true
```

`download-button` provides a shortcode for resources such as datasets or
scripts:

<pre><code>{{&lt; download-button path="../files/data.csv" text="Download dataset" filename="data.csv" &gt;}}</code></pre>

## Existing use

`structure_biology` already includes `callout-solution`,
`collapse-callouts`, and `download-button` from this extension family. It is
a concrete example of these authoring features in course material, although
its `exercise`/`solution` profile naming predates the recommended
`student`/`instructor` convention.

## Contributor guidance

When editing ordinary course content:

- Reuse extension syntax already present in nearby source documents.
- Use the teaching-tools syntax above when adding new conditional content to
  a project configured with these extensions.
- Check the corresponding student and instructor outputs where filtering is
  involved.
- Run project-provided validators for assets or scripts when relevant.
- Do not modify installed extension code simply to make a small content edit.

## Maintainer guidance

If a feature must be introduced or altered, inspect `_extensions/`, `filters/`,
profile files, output formats, and CI render commands as one change. A filter
can affect what is published even when a source document looks correct in
isolation.
